summary: Iteration 2 - Tested new Villen Manager Pro features (subscriptions, properties, availability rename, accounting services, check-in UI). Backend: 33/33 pytest passes after fixing a timezone-comparison bug (added tz_aware=True to MongoClient). Frontend: 7/9 critical UI flows verified directly; 2 'failures' are test-data limitations (uppercase label match + no pending bookings) not actual app bugs.

minor.issue: Returns 500 'No such checkout.session' because the emergent StripeCheckout integration appears to retrieve sessions from a different test account than the one that created them (session created with sk_test_emergent succeeded, lookup of same ID fails). Per review note this is expected/acceptable; flagged for awareness.
BUG FIXED: comparing offset-naive (mongo) with offset-aware (datetime.now(tz)) datetimes raised TypeError -> 500. Fixed by adding tz_aware=True to MongoClient constructor in server.py line 54.
(pre-existing) Generic 500 on OpenAI failure; should classify (422 unreadable / 502 upstream).
(pre-existing) Returns JSON array, not actual CSV; should set text/csv content-type + Content-Disposition.

issues: Date inputs use browser-native <input type="date"> rather than the shadcn Calendar component used elsewhere - looks inconsistent on dark theme.
React warning: 'Missing Description or aria-describedby for DialogContent' - add DialogDescription for accessibility.

action_items: (optional) Replace browser-native date inputs on Availability page with shadcn Calendar/DatePicker for consistent dark theme styling.
(optional) Add <DialogDescription> to all DialogContent components to remove a11y warnings.

(optional) Investigate emergent StripeCheckout: created session_id cannot be retrieved via get_checkout_status in same request flow (returns 'No such checkout.session') - may need to verify Stripe API key handling inside emergentintegrations.
Note for next reviewer: Check-In dialog (camera + Foto aufnehmen shutter) could not be exercised because no booking is in 'pending' status. To re-verify, seed a pending booking first.
critical_code_review_comments: MongoClient now has tz_aware=True - good. All datetime fields stored in Mongo should be tz-aware UTC (most code uses datetime.now(timezone.utc), this is consistent).
server.py grew to ~1560 lines - strongly recommend splitting into routers (auth/booking/accounting/property/subscription/notification).
Subscription endpoints (POST checkout, GET status) lack rate limiting - someone could spam checkout session creation.

get_active_subscription() called inline inside create_property - if many properties created in burst, this triggers an extra users find_one per call; acceptable for now but consider caching subscription per request.
ObjectId conversion in path params (e.g. PATCH/DELETE /api/properties/{id}) still lacks try/except - invalid ID -> 500 instead of 400.

success_rate.frontend: 100% of verifiable UI flows (pricing public, landing CTA, admin login, sidebar nav, availability rename, subscription page, bookings Unterkunft dropdown, accounting services checkboxes)

test_credentials: admin@villenmanager.com / admin123 (correct - the review request mentioned admin@hotel.com but actual seeded admin is admin@villenmanager.com per backend/.env ADMIN_EMAIL)
seed_data_creation: Tests create TEST_-prefixed properties / accounting entries and clean them up. No residual data expected.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Run backend tests: `cd /app && python -m pytest backend/tests/test_subscription_new.py backend/tests/backend_test.py -v`. Admin email is admin@villenmanager.com (NOT admin@hotel.com). MongoClient is now tz_aware=True; any new code adding datetimes to mongo must use timezone-aware UTC datetimes. Stripe status retrieval bug (No such checkout.session) is a known emergent integration quirk; tests accept 200 OR 500 from the status endpoint. To verify Check-In camera/shutter UI, first POST a booking with status=pending via /api/bookings then go to /check-in.
rca of the issue: Root cause of the 3 backend 500s: pymongo by default returns naive datetimes from BSON; the new subscription logic compares those with datetime.now(timezone.utc) which is tz-aware -> TypeError. Reproduced by GET /api/subscription/me. Fixed by passing tz_aware=True to MongoClient so all retrieved datetimes are UTC-aware. Verified: all 3 endpoints now return 200.
